findings.title: Focused checks assert exact dated active sprint IDs
findings.whyItMatters: One-time closeout checks are unsafe as nightly checks after rollover if they hard-fail on the current active sprint.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Acceptable only in explicitly historical closeout or migration checks.

findings.title: Foundation DB mixes schema, seed, stores, and query APIs
findings.whyItMatters: Large mixed-responsibility surfaces slow audits, increase merge risk, and make future proof harder to isolate.
findings.falsePositiveNote: This is not approval to refactor during the audit sprint.

findings.title: /api/foundation-hub is an all-in-one aggregation chokepoint
findings.whyItMatters: The first Foundation render depends on the heaviest aggregate endpoint and serial builder fanout.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Aggregator routes are fine when section timing, payload budgets, and cache boundaries are explicit.

findings.title: Foundation verifier is one large execution surface
findings.whyItMatters: Large mixed-responsibility surfaces slow audits, increase merge risk, and make future proof harder to isolate.
findings.falsePositiveNote: This is not approval to refactor during the audit sprint.

findings.title: Live-looking Current Sprint truth is hardcoded
findings.whyItMatters: Current Sprint command truth must come from live sprint records, not stale code or docs that can block rollover.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Historical closeout sections are acceptable when explicitly labeled as history.

findings.title: Live-looking Current Sprint truth is hardcoded
findings.whyItMatters: Current Sprint command truth must come from live sprint records, not stale code or docs that can block rollover.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Historical closeout sections are acceptable when explicitly labeled as history.

findings.title: KPI health probes run on source/hub request paths without a visible timeout budget
findings.whyItMatters: Sequential Supabase table/RPC probes can hold core Foundation routes hostage.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Acceptable only with cache, timeout, and stale fallback proof.

findings.title: Process/check path contains write side effects
findings.whyItMatters: Nightly audits must not execute scripts that can move backlog, reopen sprints, write reports unexpectedly, or mutate source systems without explicit apply mode.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Closeout scripts can be expected mutators, but they need explicit apply/confirm boundaries before reuse by nightly audits.

findings.title: Process/check path contains write side effects

findings.title: Scheduled Foundation jobs target scripts with mutation side effects
findings.whyItMatters: A scheduled report or verifier lane can reopen old sprints or move backlog state if command targets are not allowlisted.

findings.title: Verifier mixes active sprint assertions with historical closeout proof
findings.whyItMatters: A current-sprint advancement assertion can pass from a historical closeout unless active and historical helpers are separate.

findings.title: /api/source-of-truth risk: Latency 2136ms exceeds 2000ms budget.
findings.whyItMatters: Endpoint timing and payload budgets keep Foundation surfaces usable before code quality issues become operator drag.
findings.detector: live endpoint timing and payload measurement

findings.title: Foundation client embeds a large current-state renderer
findings.whyItMatters: Large mixed-responsibility surfaces slow audits, increase merge risk, and make future proof harder to isolate.
findings.falsePositiveNote: This is not approval to refactor during the audit sprint.

findings.title: Foundation Hub route builds many domains in one handler
findings.whyItMatters: Large mixed-responsibility surfaces slow audits, increase merge risk, and make future proof harder to isolate.
findings.falsePositiveNote: This is not approval to refactor during the audit sprint.

findings.title: GStack Build Intel snapshot recursively scans the local mirror on request
findings.whyItMatters: Request-time filesystem walking can grow without a file/depth/time cap.

findings.title: Foundation UI embeds current-state summary truth
findings.whyItMatters: Static UI copy can report stale health, source, or KPI counts after APIs change.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Static maturity explainers are acceptable; live checkpoint wording is not.

findings.title: Foundation UI embeds current-state summary truth
findings.whyItMatters: Static UI copy can report stale health, source, or KPI counts after APIs change.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Static maturity explainers are acceptable; live checkpoint wording is not.

findings.title: KPI health uses hardcoded 2026 windows
findings.whyItMatters: KPI health can silently age into stale YTD truth unless the current year comes from a source contract or runtime date rule.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Durable policy dates are acceptable when tied to an as-of source contract.

findings.title: KPI health uses hardcoded 2026 windows
findings.whyItMatters: KPI health can silently age into stale YTD truth unless the current year comes from a source contract or runtime date rule.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Durable policy dates are acceptable when tied to an as-of source contract.

findings.title: Source contract count is encoded as an exact baseline
findings.whyItMatters: Source registry additions can break or stale-pass different surfaces when expected counts are not derived from `getSourceContracts()`.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Accepted historical closeout text can stay if it is not used as current live truth.

findings.title: Source contract count is encoded as an exact baseline
findings.whyItMatters: Source registry additions can break or stale-pass different surfaces when expected counts are not derived from `getSourceContracts()`.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Accepted historical closeout text can stay if it is not used as current live truth.

findings.title: Source contract count is encoded as an exact baseline
findings.whyItMatters: Source registry additions can break or stale-pass different surfaces when expected counts are not derived from `getSourceContracts()`.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Accepted historical closeout text can stay if it is not used as current live truth.

findings.title: Source contract count is encoded as an exact baseline
findings.whyItMatters: Source registry additions can break or stale-pass different surfaces when expected counts are not derived from `getSourceContracts()`.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Accepted historical closeout text can stay if it is not used as current live truth.

findings.title: Source contract count is encoded as an exact baseline
findings.whyItMatters: Source registry additions can break or stale-pass different surfaces when expected counts are not derived from `getSourceContracts()`.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Accepted historical closeout text can stay if it is not used as current live truth.

findings.title: Read/report paths can call seed-mutating DB initialization
findings.whyItMatters: Read-only audit commands need schema-readiness checks that do not silently sync seed rows.

findings.title: Foundation ships large frontend assets under no-store cache policy
findings.whyItMatters: Every refresh can redownload and reparse the full control-panel bundle and stylesheet.

findings.title: Source Lifecycle route loads the full Foundation snapshot
findings.whyItMatters: A source-specific page pays for archive, candidate, synthesis, jobs, runtime, Drive, backlog, and audit surfaces before rendering one lifecycle payload.

findings.title: Admin deal-review policy dates are duplicated across runner, job config, and UI
findings.whyItMatters: Policy cutoff changes require manual edits across several surfaces unless a source contract owns the dates.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Durable policy dates are acceptable when source ID, owner, and as-of date are explicit.

findings.title: Plan Critic 9.8 threshold appears as raw literals
findings.whyItMatters: Approval policy can drift when some checks import the registry constant and others hardcode the score.
findings.falsePositiveNote: The canonical constant is valid; duplicated raw literals are the risk.

findings.title: Build closeout history is code-owned data
findings.whyItMatters: Large mixed-responsibility surfaces slow audits, increase merge risk, and make future proof harder to isolate.
findings.falsePositiveNote: This is not approval to refactor during the audit sprint.

findings.title: Build Intel context search can fetch and scan large artifact text
findings.whyItMatters: ILIKE filters over title/content and JS excerpt scoring can become a full-table text scan as artifacts grow.

findings.title: Build Log shells out to git and returns duplicate group/build payloads
findings.whyItMatters: Request-time subprocess work and duplicated serialized build entries can make Recent Work slow as closeout history grows.

findings.title: /api/foundation-hub warning: Payload 874776 bytes exceeds 800KB watch budget.
findings.whyItMatters: Endpoint timing and payload budgets keep Foundation surfaces usable before code quality issues become operator drag.
findings.detector: live endpoint timing and payload measurement

findings.title: Build Intel commit is pinned as expected truth
findings.whyItMatters: A fixed inspected commit is valid snapshot evidence, but it must not be treated as latest Build Intel monitoring truth.
findings.falsePositiveNote: Valid when labeled inspected snapshot evidence with an as-of date.

findings.title: Foundation frontend has heavy DOM rebuild signals
findings.whyItMatters: Backlog and source filters can churn large DOM trees on each interaction as card/source counts grow.

findings.title: Global overflow hiding can mask layout failures
findings.whyItMatters: A missed table wrapper can clip content instead of visibly failing during review.

llmReview.note: An approved route is available; V1 still records packets by default unless an explicit live LLM review mode is enabled.
